# Pull Request

## 📋 Description

<!-- Provide a clear description of your changes -->

## 🎯 Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (fixes an issue with course materials)
- [ ] New content (adds new examples, resources, or sections)
- [ ] Content update (improves existing materials)
- [ ] Documentation (updates to README, CONTRIBUTING, etc.)
- [ ] Code examples (adds or improves code snippets)
- [ ] Frontend (changes to the Next.js app)
- [ ] Other: _____

## 📍 Files Changed

<!-- List the main files you've modified -->

- `file-name.md` - Description of changes

## ✅ Checklist

<!-- Mark completed items with [x] -->

### Content Standards
- [ ] Followed the file numbering convention (000-099, 100-199, etc.)
- [ ] Used beginner-friendly language
- [ ] Included prompting framework examples (where applicable)
- [ ] Added section markers to code examples
- [ ] Specified file locations for code snippets
- [ ] Used checkbox format `- [ ]` for actionable items
- [ ] Checked all external links work

### Code Examples (if applicable)
- [ ] Code is tested and works
- [ ] Includes structured comments and section markers
- [ ] Follows format: `languagename-section-number-theme`
- [ ] Specifies which file and directory code belongs in
- [ ] Uses modern best practices (HTML5, ES6+, Python 3.x)
- [ ] Includes accessibility considerations

### Documentation
- [ ] Updated relevant README sections
- [ ] Added/updated links in Table of Contents
- [ ] Proper markdown formatting
- [ ] Spell-checked content

## 🧪 Testing

<!-- Describe how you tested your changes -->

- [ ] Reviewed all changed files for accuracy
- [ ] Tested any code examples
- [ ] Verified all links work
- [ ] Checked markdown renders correctly

## 📚 For Reviewers

**Please check:**
- [ ] Content maintains beginner-friendly tone
- [ ] Examples follow the prompting framework
- [ ] File numbering is correct
- [ ] No breaking changes to existing materials
- [ ] Changes align with course objectives
